div1.entryFree:
Ban, subst. 1) curse: take thou that too, with multiplying --s, Tim. IV, 1, 34. with Hecate's b. thrice blasted, Hml. III, 2, 269. sometime with lunatic --s, sometime with prayers, enforce their charity, Lr. II, 3, 19.
2) only in the plural, bans (O. Edd. banes) == notice of a matrimonial contract proclaimed in the church: when I shall ask the --s and when be married, Shr. II, 181. make feasts, invite friends, and proclaim the --s, III, 2, 16. contracted bachelors, such as had been asked twice on the --s, H4A IV, 2, 18. I, her husband, contradict your --s, Lr. V, 3, 87.
